name: PACT
description: Protocol for Agent-human Collaborative Trust. A lightweight TypeScript protocol that lets AI agents earn autonomy through human feedback, with trust attestations recorded onchain on Base. Agents surface decisions based on a stakes x trust classification matrix, learn from human responses, detect patterns, and create auto-approve rules over time.
author: Julian Hutton

TrustAttestation.description: Records approval/rejection attestations with trust scores. Any agent can call verifyTrust(agentId, minScore) to check another agent's track record.

AgentRegistry.description: Onchain agent identity. Agents register with a name and description, queryable by any other agent.

tools.name: trust-classification
tools.description: Classifies agent decisions using a stakes x trust matrix to determine autonomy level (auto-approve, inform, request, blocked)

tools.name: trust-scoring
tools.description: Updates agent trust scores based on human feedback: approved (+2), edited (+1), rejected (-5)

tools.name: pattern-detection
tools.description: Detects repeated approval patterns and surfaces meta-decisions to auto-approve similar future actions

tools.name: onchain-attestation
tools.description: Records trust attestations onchain on Base via the TrustAttestation contract

tools.name: agent-registry
tools.description: Registers and queries agent identities onchain via the AgentRegistry contract

tools.name: trust-verification
tools.description: Permissionless onchain verification of any agent's trust score by other agents

examples.name: quickstart
examples.description: Full lifecycle in ~50 lines: surface, classify, resolve, learn

examples.name: deploy-approval
examples.description: CI/CD agent earning trust over production deploys with pattern detection

examples.name: multi-agent-trust
examples.description: Orchestrator ranks worker agents by trust, delegates high-stakes tasks

examples.name: trading-agent
examples.description: Trading agent with risk thresholds and learned auto-approve rules
